stories.title: Colorado Renaissance Festival Opens June 13-14 — Opening Weekend Guide
stories.angle: The 49th season of the Colorado Renaissance Festival opens this Saturday, June 13, in Larkspur — roughly 30 minutes from Colorado Springs via I-25. It runs every Saturday and Sunday through August 2, plus the July 4 holiday weekend. The opening weekend (June 13-14) is "Magical Fantasy Weekend," themed around fantasy creatures of all kinds. It's also First Responder Appreciation Weekend: firefighters, police, and sheriff's department personnel receive BOGO adult admission, and their children under 12 get in free. King Soopers carries the best-priced advance tickets: $29 adults, $14 kids 5-12, under 5 free. Located at 650 W Perry Park Ave, Larkspur, on a 350-acre forested site the festival has called home since 1976.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Colorado Renaissance Festival opens THIS Saturday in Larkspur.

June 13-14: Magical Fantasy Weekend + First Responder Appreciation. BOGO adult tickets for first responders, kids under 12 free.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
King Soopers has the best ticket price: $29 adults, $14 kids 5-12, under 5 free.

Day-of tickets cost more. Opens June 13 in Larkspur, runs Sat & Sun through Aug 2.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

stories.title: COS Public Pools and Free Splash Pads — Summer 2026 Guide
stories.angle: With Portal Pool years from reopening, Colorado Springs families have three affordable or free ways to stay cool this summer. Two YMCA-partnership outdoor pools are open Memorial Day through Labor Day — no membership required. Wilson Ranch Pool is open Monday through Sunday, 10 AM to 6 PM; day passes are $10 for children and $15 for adults. Monument Valley Pool has the same pricing with Sunday hours starting at noon. Three free spray grounds operate across the city: Deerfield Hills Spray Ground (southeast COS, 50+ spray nozzles, 16 water features), the Watering Hole at Venezia Park (northeast COS, universally accessible playground adjacent), and the Splash Pad at Panorama Park (southeast COS, daily 10 AM-6 PM). Great Wolf Lodge offers indoor water park day passes for families wanting a larger experience.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
COS summer cooling: 3 free splash pads + 2 low-cost pools (no YMCA membership needed).

Wilson Ranch + Monument Valley: $10 kids, $15 adults. Free: Deerfield Hills, Venezia Park, Panorama Park.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

stories.title: CPSC Pool Safety Recall: Drain Covers and Above-Ground Pools
stories.angle: Two separate CPSC recalls issued in June 2026 affect pool equipment that many Colorado Springs families may own as summer swim season peaks. First: Broqixin pool drain covers (sold by seller Eicong on Amazon) violate the Virginia Graeme Baker Pool and Spa Safety Act and pose entrapment and drowning hazards — pool owners should stop using their pools immediately and contact Eicong for a full refund. Second: Blue Wave above-ground pools that are 48 inches tall or taller have been recalled due to a drowning hazard. If you set up a new pool or bought pool equipment this spring, now is the time to check model numbers against CPSC.gov.

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text:
CPSC recall: Broqixin pool drain covers (sold on Amazon by Eicong) violate federal pool safety law and pose an entrapment and drowning hazard.

Stop using your pool immediately and contact Eicong for a full refund.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text:
Blue Wave above-ground pools 48 inches or taller have also been recalled by the CPSC due to a drowning hazard.

If you set up a new pool this spring, check the brand and model number at CPSC.gov before the kids get in.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

stories.title: PPYMCA Summer Day Camp Week 3 — Tech Week June 15-19
stories.angle: Pikes Peak YMCA Summer Day Camp's Week 3 (June 15-19) goes all in on technology. Campers ages 5-12 will explore music production and songwriting with the Colorado Springs Conservatory, movie making and animation with Snapology, and programming and coding with the Space Foundation. It's a creative, hands-on tech week designed to show kids how technology powers entertainment and problem-solving. Registration at ppymca.org; financial assistance is available through the YMCA scholarship program.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
PPYMCA Summer Day Camp Week 3 (June 15-19) is tech week for ages 5-12.

Music with COS Conservatory, movies with Snapology, coding with the Space Foundation.

Register at ppymca.org. Financial aid available.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

stories.title: Tiny Land Play Tent Recall — Fiberglass Poles Pose Skin and Eye Risk
stories.angle: Tiny Land children's play tents sold on Amazon and Tinylandus.com between November 2025 and March 2026 have been recalled by the CPSC due to fiberglass poles that can shed fibers, causing skin and eye irritation. The tents sold for $54 to $70 each. Eight incidents of skin or eye irritation from the fiber shedding have been reported; no serious injuries. Remedy: stop using the tent immediately, disassemble it, and keep the poles away from children. Contact Tiny Land to receive a free repair kit with four replacement plastic poles, including shipping.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
CPSC recall: Tiny Land play tents (Amazon, Nov 2025-Mar 2026, $54-$70) have fiberglass poles that shed fibers — skin/eye irritation risk.

Stop using. Free plastic replacement kit at tinylandus.com/pages/recalls.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting
